id.description: Unique identifier for this mod. Must be globally unique within all mods from all loaders.

loaderId.description: ID of the mod loader required to use this mod. If not set, assumed to be compatible with any loader.

isLoader.description: Whether this mod is a mod loader, meaning it is required to install one of these to be able to use any of the mods in this library.

deprecated.description: If true, users should be warned about it and invited to uninstall this mod, and directed to install the appropriate replacement.

redownloadId.description: Increment this number to force clients to redownload this mod. Useful when a mod needs to be redownloaded without a new version being released.

engine.description: Game engine that this mod supports.

architecture.description: Whether this mod is only compatible with 64-bit or 32-bit (x86) games. If not set, assumed to be compatible with both.

unityBackend.description: Only relevant if engine=Unity. Scripting backend used by the game.

sourceCode.description: URL of a repository with the mod's source code.

description.description: Short description that explains what the mod does.

root.description: Optional, path of the root folder within the zip.

configs.description: Information used to find local mod configs, and also for downloading configs from the database.

destinationPath.description: Path in the user's PC where the config is installed to. This path can be relative to something, depending on the mod loader.

destinationType.description: If file, the downloaded config will be placed directly in the installTo path. If folder, the downloaded file must be a zip, and gets extracted to the destination path, which must be a folder.

modIdOverride.description: If set, overrides the mod ID used for finding config files. Useful when multiple mods use the same configs.

dependencies.description: Mods that need to be installed before this one.

modId.description: ID of the mod to depend on.

operatingSystems.description: Dependency only gets installed if the user is on one of these operating systems. If not set, assumed to be required for all.
